author: luca_zanolini
content:
Hi! A question on the slashing mechanism that clears builder IOUs.

It seems to me that process_proposer_slashing decides which builder IOU to clear based *only* on the slot number in the slashing evidence (header_1.slot), not on who actually proposed that slot. There's no check that the slashed validator was slot N's proposer.

So it looks like any slashable validator V can double-sign two headers claiming slot = N (V does not have to be slot N's proposer, and the headers do not have to be canonical blocks). If the resulting valid ProposerSlashing is included on chain while slot N is still in the 2-epoch payment window, it zeroes the entry holding the honest proposer's IOU for slot N, griefing an unrelated builder payment (proposer never credited, builder never charged) for the cost of one slashing.

Is this intentional, or should the clear also require header_1.proposer_index to match slot N's expected proposer before zeroing?

author: potuz
content: Oh wow this is a very nice catch! Didn't even think about this grief vector. It's not so brutal though cause this only applies for Payloads that weren't revealed but it's severe enough that justifies keeping a validator to slash itself whenever you make a large free option bet for example. Care to write the fix? It's a very nice one

author: luca_zanolini
content:
https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5365

please review when you get a chance! 🙏🏻

author: potuz
content: Thanks, when I read this morning I thought a little on how to do this without modifying the structure and it doesn' t look that nice. This solution works for me as far as I can see. It' s not that inconvenient to modify this structure. You need to add tests and probably modify the other forks as well.
